subject: english
text:
Choose a correct phrasal verb.
A) The bombs did not hurt anyone, but they were meant to ________ (wake up/wake/woke) the nation.
B) He was the only person ------------- (Waiting for/waited for) the day of death.
C) He was -------------- (longing for/longing to) the better change.
D) The revolutionaries used violence to ________ (throw of/ throw off )the foreign rule.

subject: english
text:
Choose the  right answer for the following.
A) He chose to die for a cause rather than ________ (live/living/lived) a long life without purpose.
B) His courage and clarity of thought were ________ (admire/admirable/admirably).
C) He wrote several ________ (power/powerful/powerfully) letters from prison.
D) His sacrifice was not in ________ (vain/vein/vane).
e.  ________ (Despite/In spite/Although) being young, he had mature political ideas.
f. He believed that the pen was mightier than the ________ (sword/sward/word).
g. Many people regard him as a ________ (nation/national/nationalist) hero.
